session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8842 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the provided arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill using the arguments provided:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --stat -M -C origin/main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "Unable to compute diff stats"
Get file restructuring context via git diff

Found 2 violations across 2 files. Inline comment posted for 1 (1 pre-existing on unchanged line noted in summary only).

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ Verbatim source code blocks — None found (previous verbatim violations in git-plumbing-patterns.md were already resolved)
✅ Inaccurate claims — None found
✅ Duplicative sections — None found
❌ Drift risk sections — Found in rebase-conflict-patterns.md:64 (private method name)
❌ Drift risk sections — Found in workflow-run-list.md:20-21 (line counts, pre-existing, on unchanged lines — could not post inline)

### Violations Summary
- `docs/learned/architecture/rebase-conflict-patterns.md:64`: Private method `_is_graphite_enabled()` referenced in prose — high drift risk (inline comment posted)
- `docs/learned/cli/workflow-run-list.md:20-21`: Line counts ("192 lines", "42 lines") in Source Files section — go stale on every edit (pre-existing; on unchanged lines, could not post inline comment)

</details>", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8842#issuecomment-4012565127" }, { "id": 4015011957, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- erk:pr-address-run -->
## PR Review Comments Addressed

This PR was updated by an automated process triggered by:
```
erk pr address-remote 8842
```

### What is this?

[Erk](https://github.com/schrockn/erk) is a CLI tool for plan-oriented agentic
engineering. The `pr-address` workflow uses Claude to read PR review comments
and address them automatically.

### Changes Made

The changes replace inline code examples with references to the actual source code locations, making the documentation easier to maintain. Instead of duplicating Python code snippets that could become outdated, the docs now point readers to the relevant functions in the codebase and describe their behavior in prose. This approach reduces documentation drift while keeping explanations clear and accurate.

### Details

- **Model:** claude-opus-4-6
- **Workflow run:** [View details](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22787959265)
- **Commits added:** 2

---
*Automated by erk pr-address workflow*", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8842#issuecomment-4015011957"
